ref: From there, Jesus went along Lake Galilee. Then he climbed a hill and sat down.
text: Afei Yesu firii hɔ bɛfaa Galilea po he ho, na ɔkɔɔ mmepɔ he so, ne ɔtenaa ase.
translated: After Jesus had left Galilee and gone to the other side, he went up the mountain and sat down

ref: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
text: Ɔka kyerɛɛ no sɛ, “Mu deɛ ɔya he?” Na Yesu kaa sɛ, “Nni awu; Nsɛe awareɛ; Mmɔ korɔno; Nni adansekunumu;
translated: And he asked them, “What are you discussing?” And Jesus said, “I am the resurrection; I am life; I am the one who has come from heaven; I am the bread of life;

ref: There were also James and John, the two sons of Zebedee. Jesus called them Boanerges, which means “Thunderbolts.”
text: Sebedeo ba Yakubu ɔne Yohane a ɔyɛ Yakubu nua ;
translated: The brothers of James and John who are the sons of Zebedee

ref: Jesus went to the home of Peter, where he found that Peter's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Na afei Yesu kɔɔ Petro fie, na abunu akye Petro ase baa da hɔ.
translated: And when Jesus went to Peter's house, he saw Peter's mother-in-law lying down with a fever.

ref: and told his officials, “This is John the Baptist! He has come back from death, and that's why he has the power to work these miracles.”
text: na ɔka kyerɛɛ ne nkoa sɛ, “Yohane Suboni di; wanyane afiri awufoɔ mu, saa nti ne ahoɔdenneɛ nnwuma yi ɔɔda adi wɔ ne mu he.”
translated: And he ordered his servant to say, “Take away the stone; the one who was dead will come out, for his miraculous work is completed in him.”
